references.text: Chantanaorrathai, S & A Chantanaorrapint. 2012. A new species record of Sciaphila (Triuridaceae) for Thailand. Thai Forest Bulletin (Botany) 40: 114-117
Meerendonk, JPM de. 1984. Triuridaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 10: 109-121
Mennes, CB, EF Smets, SN Moses & VSFT Merckx. 2013. New insights in the long-debated evolutionary history of Triuridaceae (Pandanales). Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution 69: 994-1004
Suetsugu, K, M Dancák, M Hrones, A Naiki, S Tagane & T Yahara. 2020. Resurrection and emended description of Sciaphila major (Triuridaceae). Phytotaxa 459: 25-38.
Suetsugu, K. 2018. Sciaphila lambirensis (Triuridaceae), a new mycoheterotrophic plant from Sarawak, Borneo, Malaysia. Acta Phytotaxonomica et Geobotanica 69: 175-180

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 December 2019

species.citation: Becc. , Malesia 3 (1890) 337 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 117. = Sciaphila clemensae Hemsl. , Hooker's Icon. Pl. 29 (1907) t.2850 ; Fedde Repert. 5 (1908) 265; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 28; Type: Clemens 775.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra. MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS.

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1851) 322 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 120.
species.distribution: Pacific Ocean, Philippines. LUZON: NCR, MINDANAO.

species.citation: Schltr. , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 49 (1912) 87 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 118.
species.distribution: Borneo, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka. LUZON: NCR.

species.citation: Miers , Proc. Linn. Soc. London 2 (1850) 73 ; S.Vidal, Phan. Cuming. Philipp. (1885) 154; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 282; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 28; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 113.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea. LUZON: Bataan, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Zamboanga.
species.notes: Meerendonk omits the Philippines in the distribution range of this species, but Merrill listed the cited Philippine localities; in damp primary forests at low and medium elevation. 100-1200m.

species.citation: Giesen , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (18) (1938) 49 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 120. = Sciaphila mindanaensis Giesen , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV (18) (1938) 51.
species.distribution: New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines. MINDANAO: Davao del Sur.

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1851) 322 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 117.
species.distribution: Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Laguna.

species.citation: Thwaites ex Benth. , Hooker's J. Bot. Kew Gard. Misc. 7 (1855) 10 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 116.
species.distribution: Borneo, Hong Kong, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sumatra. LUZON: Aurora, NEGROS (photos).

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 515 ; Meerend., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1984) 113.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Singapore, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Aurora (photos), MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur.
